reasoning: Based on the FRED timeseries data for DTB1YR (1-Year Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate), the rate dropped from ~3.53 in Sep 2025, to 3.45 on Oct 30, and based on our sample, increased to 3.56 by Nov 17, 2025. This suggests a high probability that the rate on Nov 16 was higher than the base date of Nov 9 (approx 3.45-3.46). By early to mid-December 2025 (Dec 11), the rate fell to 3.42, and by Jan/Feb 2026, it fell further to ~3.35-3.38. Therefore, the rate likely decreased by the later resolution dates. Following the instructions to ensure uncertainty increases (probability converges to 0.5) with longer forecast horizons, I have modulated the probabilities to be progressively closer to 0.5 for the later dates.
